C. Scoring Subsequences
time limit per test
2 seconds
memory limit per test
256 megabytes
input
standard input
output
standard output

The score of a sequence $$$[a_1, a_2, \dots, a_k]$$$ is defined as $$$\sum\limits_{i=1}^{k} a_i \cdot i$$$.

Given a sequence of $$$n$$$ integers, for every prefix of length $$$c$$$ (with $$$1 \le c \le n$$$) find the maximum score over all subsequences of length $$$c$$$. Note that $$$a_i \le 10^9$$$ for all $$$i$$$.

Input

The first line contains a single integer $$$t$$$ ($$$1 \le t \le 1000$$$) — the number of test cases.

Each test case consists of two lines. The first contains $$$n$$$ ($$$1 \le n \le 2 \cdot 10^5$$$); the second contains $$$n$$$ integers.

Output

For each test case, output $$$n$$$ integers.

Example
Input
                  
2

                  
3

                  
1 2 3

                  
4

                  
10 20 30 40
Output
3 5 10
40 90 140 200
Input
1
5
5 4 3 2 1
Output
5 9 12 14 15
Note

In the first test case, the answer for $$$c = 1$$$ is $$$3$$$.
